headline: Microsoft (MSFT) Faces Browser Scrutiny As New York Clouds Data Center Expansion
summary: Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT) is under fresh antitrust pressure after the Browser Choice Alliance accused the company of using browser bundling and dark patterns in Windows to steer users toward its Edge browser. New York has issued an executive order placing a moratorium on new large scale data centers, creating uncertainty for Microsoft’s planned AI focused infrastructure buildout in the state. Other US states are reported to be considering similar restrictions on data centers, which could...

headline: ValorC3 Data Centers Launches Backup as a Service (BaaS) to Safeguard Critical Enterprise Data
summary: ValorC3 Data Centers today announced the general availability of Backup as a Service, a fully managed offering that protects the SaaS data businesses rely on most, including Microsoft 365, Entra ID and Salesforce. Every backup is immutable, so data stays recoverable after deletion, corruption or a ransomware attack.

content.title: ValorC3 Data Centers Launches Backup as a Service (BaaS) to Safeguard Critical Enterprise Data
content.summary: ValorC3 Data Centers today announced the general availability of Backup as a Service, a fully managed offering that protects the SaaS data businesses rely on most, including Microsoft 365, Entra ID and Salesforce. Every backup is immutable, so data stays recoverable after deletion, corruption or a ransomware attack.

thumbnail.caption: ValorC3 Data Centers, formerly Tonaquint, has transitioned to strategically align with the company’s mission to expand modern colocation, cloud and connectivity services across emerging U.S. markets. The ‘C3’ highlights Valor’s dedication to delivering dependable infrastructure across three core pillars – colocation, cloud and connectivity.

headline: The Surprising Tech Rotation Boosting Stocks This Earnings Season
summary: U.S. stocks remain deep in the throes of a major rotation heading into the teeth of the second-quarter earnings season, and the traditional August lull that follows, as investors take money from highflying tech sector and spread that cash across a host of old-economy bets.  What’s happening alongside that “great rotation,” however, is also interesting, and likely keeping stocks afloat amid the broader tech declines.

headline: Are companies getting a return on investment from AI yet?
summary: Are industries starting to see a return on investment (ROI) from AI services? JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon told Business Insider that while AI has helped reduce jobs in certain areas of the major bank, it hasn't contributed toward shrinking operating costs. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Business Insider Today executive editor Dan DeFrancesco to talk more about how companies are using AI to boost efficiency.

content.title: Are companies getting a return on investment from AI yet?
